data.content:
The bug is on line 29 — `ProcessPayment` only checks for zero, not negative amounts. The fastest workflow here is `dotnet watch test`, which watches for file changes and automatically re-runs tests (incremental rebuild, no manual steps).

Let me set it up and fix the bug:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for zero but not negative amounts. The fix is to add a negative amount check. 

The user is asking about the fastest workflow to iterate. The answer is `dotnet watch test` — it watches for file changes and re-runs tests automatically without a full rebuild. Let me set that up.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for the failing test

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8e9c23ba59bf4708a6b2ae5a85025223 && dotnet watch test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for the failing test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}
